articles.title: OpenAI Launches Full-Scale Effort to Patch Open-Source Bugs as It Takes on Anthropic’s Mythos
articles.subtitle: Amid concerns about AI models’ cybersecurity capabilities, OpenAI revealed an improved version of GPT-5.5-Cyber and its “Patch the Planet” initiative to fix open-source software bugs.
articles.author: Lily Hay Newman
articles.summary: Amid concerns about AI models’ cybersecurity capabilities, OpenAI revealed an improved version of GPT-5.5-Cyber and its “Patch the Planet” initiative to fix open-source software bugs.
body: As fears about AI hacking capabilities grow, OpenAI on Monday made a slew of cybersecurity-focused announcements , including an improved version of its limited-access security-specialized model GPT-5.5-Cyber, expanded international work with governments and other institutions to give them “trusted access” to the company's latest cybersecurity-focused models, and releasing its Codex Security scanner as an app plug-in.

As advances across the AI industry leave critical open-source projects at increasing risk of falling behind, though, the company also said on Monday that it is launching an effort known as Patch the Planet, founded with the prominent research-focused security firm Trail of Bits and in collaboration with vulnerability management firms HackerOne and Calif.

The project has already begun its work offering free security consulting services to open source maintainers to not only help them find and patch vulnerabilities, but also support them in strengthening their code bases and incorporating AI security tools into their development process. The idea is to give individualized support to as many open-source projects as possible to improve both their current security and long-term resilience in a way that will actually be sustainable.

“Patch the Planet is an internet-scale effort to help open-source software get ahead of AI bug-hunting tools,” says Trail of Bits CEO and cofounder Dan Guido. “But it's also an effort to help the open-source community see the benefits and not just the downsides of AI coding tools.”

Open-source developers—typically volunteers keeping critical and widely used software afloat with few resources—are often already struggling to keep up with bug reports. The rise of AI vulnerability hunting in recent months has, for many maintainers, made that backlog feel insurmountable as AI-generated slop reports stack up, making it difficult to prioritize and pulling already limited time and attention away from critical flaws.

Maintainers “do their work out of love of open source, and now they’re stuck reviewing slop CVEs,” says OpenAI's cyber tech lead, Fouad Matin. With Patch the Planet, he says, “what we’ve effectively done is make it as efficient from a token perspective as possible to reduce the burden for maintainers—code base assessments, validating potential reports, creating patches, and landing them. We want to offset costs, whether it's tokens or people power, to actually patch as much of the world of software as possible.”

Matin adds that for its Codex Security scanner, which has been in research preview since earlier this year, OpenAI has been subsidizing usage for both open-source and private code “to the tune of 20 trillion tokens.”

More than 30 open-source projects are already participating in Patch the Planet, with more in the pipeline to start. To launch the project, Trail of Bits recently conducted a five-day opening sprint in which it had 25 engineers, or roughly a fifth of its workforce, simultaneously working on collaborations with an array of maintainers. OpenAI and Trail of Bits say the project has already uncovered hundreds of bugs and produced dozens of patches in just its first week. And Guido says that with funding from OpenAI as well as unmetered model access, Trail of Bits plans to continue its intense commitment to Patch the Planet work long-term.

“It’s so rare that we get the opportunity to work on large-scale open-source security issues,” Guido says. “And Patch the Planet is not a one-size-fits-all. We speak to all the maintainers for every single project and figure out what their highest priorities are, whether it’s building better testing infrastructure or custom fuzzers or just cleaning up technical data across the project because that’s what’s going to make them work faster and operate faster and patch faster.”

Monday's announcements by OpenAI come as its competitor Anthropic had to pull its new Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models off the market earlier this month amid fear from the Trump administration about AI cybersecurity capabilities . The White House decision to hit Anthropic with export controls on the models came after the company publicly released the Mythos-grade Fable 5 with blocks on its advanced biological and cybersecurity capabilities—protections the administration feared were not adequate.

OpenAI's announcements on Monday, including the new checkpoint of GPT-5.5-Cyber, are all part of the company's limited Trusted Access for Cyber program and do not involve a public release. But with both Anthropic and OpenAI preparing for IPOs, competition clearly continues regardless of which products are currently on the market. In its GPT-5.5-Cyber announcement, for example, OpenAI points out that the model scores 85.6 percent on the benchmark assessment known as CyberGym, an improvement from a previous version of GPT-5.5-Cyber. The performance also beats Anthropic's Mythos 5, which scored 83.8 percent.

Amid this AI cybersecurity race, the Five Eyes intelligence alliance warned in an unusual joint statement on Monday that “frontier AI models are anticipated to exceed current industry expectations, fundamentally transforming both offensive and defensive cyber capabilities. The timeline is not years, it is months … In this environment, cyber resilience is integral.”
keyPoints: As fears about AI hacking capabilities grow, OpenAI on Monday made a slew of cybersecurity-focused announcements , including an improved version of its limited-access security-specialized model GPT-5.5-Cyber, expanded international work with governments and other institutions to give them “trusted access” to the company's latest cybersecurity-focused models, and releasing its Codex Security scanner as an app plug-in.

As advances across the AI industry leave critical open-source projects at increasing risk of falling behind, though, the company also said on Monday that it is launching an effort known as Patch the Planet, founded with the prominent research-focused security firm Trail of Bits and in collaboration with vulnerability management firms HackerOne and Calif.

The project has already begun its work offering free security consulting services to open source maintainers to not only help them find and patch vulnerabilities, but also support them in strengthening their code bases and incorporating AI security tools into their development process. The idea is to give individualized support to as many open-source projects as possible to improve both their current security and long-term resilience in a way that will actually be sustainable.
paragraphTranslations: 随着对人工智能黑客能力的担忧日益增长，OpenAI在周一宣布了一系列以网络安全为重点的公告，包括推出其有限访问的安全专业模型GPT-5.5-Cyber的改进版本，扩大与政府和其他机构的国际合作，以提供对公司最新网络安全模型的“可信访问”，并将其Codex安全扫描器作为应用程序插件发布。
然而，随着人工智能行业的进展使关键的开源项目面临落后的风险，公司还在周一表示，它正在启动一个名为Patch the Planet的项目，该项目由著名的研究型安全公司Trail of Bits创立，并与漏洞管理公司HackerOne和加州合作。
该项目已经开始工作，为开源维护者提供免费的安全咨询服务，不仅要帮助他们发现和修补漏洞，还要支持他们加强代码库，并将AI安全工具纳入开发流程。这个想法是尽可能为更多的开源项目提供个性化支持，以改善他们当前的安全性和长期韧性，这种方式实际上是可持续的。
“Patch the Planet是一个互联网规模的努力，帮助开源软件领先于AI漏洞狩猎工具，”Trail of Bits的首席执行官和联合创始人Dan Guido说。“但这也是一个努力，帮助开源社区看到AI编码工具的好处，而不仅仅是缺点。”
开源开发者——通常是志愿者，他们用有限的资源维持着关键且广泛使用的软件——通常已经在努力跟上错误报告。近几个月来，AI漏洞狩猎的兴起，对于许多维护者来说，使得积压的问题变得难以克服，因为AI生成的漏洞报告堆积如山，使得难以优先处理，并从关键缺陷中分散了已经有限的时间和精力。
维护者“出于对开源的热爱而工作，现在他们却陷入了审查漏洞CVE的困境，”OpenAI的网络技术负责人Fouad Matin说。有了Patch the Planet，他说，“我们实际上已经尽可能地从代币的角度提高了效率，以减轻维护者的负担——代码库评估、验证潜在报告、创建补丁和落地。我们想要抵消成本，无论是代币还是人力，以尽可能多地修补软件世界。”
Matin补充说，对于其Codex安全扫描器，自今年早些时候开始研究预览以来，OpenAI一直在为开源和私有代码的使用提供补贴，“达到了20万亿个代币。”

已经有超过30个开源项目参与了Patch the Planet，还有更多的项目即将开始。为了启动该项目，Trail of Bits最近进行了为期五天的开幕式冲刺，在此期间，它有25名工程师，大约是其劳动力的五分之一，同时与一系列维护者合作。OpenAI和Trail of Bits表示，该项目在第一周就已经发现了数百个漏洞，并产生了数十个补丁。Guido说，有了OpenAI的资金支持以及无限制的模型访问权限，Trail of Bits计划长期继续其对Patch the Planet工作的强烈承诺。
“我们很少有机会处理大规模的开源安全问题，”Guido说。“而Patch the Planet并不是一劳永逸的解决方案。我们与每个项目的维护者交谈，了解他们的最高优先事项是什么，无论是建立更好的测试基础设施还是定制的模糊测试工具，还是仅仅清理项目中的技术数据，因为这就是让他们工作更快、运行更快、修补更快的关键。”
OpenAI周一的公告是在竞争对手Anthropic不得不在本月早些时候将其新的Fable 5和Mythos 5模型撤出市场之际发布的，原因是特朗普政府对AI网络安全能力的担忧。白宫决定对Anthropic的模型实施出口管制，是在该公司公开发布了Mythos级别的Fable 5之后，该模型限制了其先进的生物和网络安全能力——政府担心这些保护措施不够充分。

OpenAI周一的公告，包括新的GPT-5.5-Cyber检查点，都是公司有限的Trusted Access for Cyber计划的一部分，不涉及公开发布。但是，随着Anthropic和OpenAI都在准备进行IPO，无论当前市场上有哪些产品，竞争显然仍在继续。例如，在GPT-5.5-Cyber的公告中，OpenAI指出，该模型在被称为CyberGym的基准评估中得分85.6%，比GPT-5.5-Cyber的先前版本有所提高。性能也超过了Anthropic的Mythos 5，后者得分为83.8%。
在这场AI网络安全竞赛中，五眼情报联盟在周一的一份不同寻常的联合声明中警告说，“前沿AI模型预计将超出当前行业的预期，根本性地改变进攻和防御网络能力。时间表不是几年，而是几个月……在这个环境中，网络韧性是不可或缺的。”

paragraphs: OpenAI has announced a range of cybersecurity initiatives, including an enhanced GPT-5.5-Cyber model, international collaboration for 'trusted access' to its AI models, and the release of its Codex Security scanner as an app plugin. The company is also launching 'Patch the Planet', a project with Trail of Bits and others, to provide free security consulting to open-source maintainers, helping them find and patch vulnerabilities and integrate AI security tools. This initiative aims to improve the security and resilience of open-source software. OpenAI's Codex Security scanner has been subsidized for both open-source and private code, and the Patch the Planet project has already identified hundreds of bugs and produced dozens of patches in its first week.

OpenAI's efforts come amidst competition with Anthropic, which recently removed its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models from the market due to concerns over AI cybersecurity capabilities. OpenAI's GPT-5.5-Cyber model scored higher on the CyberGym benchmark than Anthropic's Mythos 5, indicating its superior performance.
paragraphTranslations: OpenAI 宣布了一系列网络安全举措，包括增强版的 GPT-5.5-Cyber 模型、国际合作以获得对其 AI 模型的“可信访问”，以及将其 Codex 安全扫描器作为应用程序插件发布。该公司还启动了“Patch the Planet”项目，与 Trail of Bits 等合作，为开源维护者提供免费的安全咨询服务，帮助他们发现和修补漏洞，并整合 AI 安全工具。这一举措旨在提高开源软件的安全性和弹性。OpenAI 的 Codex 安全扫描器已经为开源和私有代码提供了补贴，而 Patch the Planet 项目在第一周就已经识别出数百个漏洞并产生了数十个补丁。
OpenAI 的努力是在与 Anthropic 的竞争中进行的，后者最近因对 AI 网络安全能力的担忧而将其 Fable 5 和 Mythos 5 模型撤出市场。OpenAI 的 GPT-5.5-Cyber 模型在 CyberGym 基准测试中的得分高于 Anthropic 的 Mythos 5，显示出其优越的性能。

articles.title: Report: Kennedy Space Center not ready for era of super heavy rockets
articles.subtitle: SpaceX has told NASA it plans to launch Starship every eight days from Kennedy.
articles.author: Eric Berger
articles.summary: SpaceX has told NASA it plans to launch Starship every eight days from Kennedy.
body: SpaceX has told NASA it plans to launch Starship every eight days from Kennedy.

NASA’s infrastructure at Kennedy Space Center, the crown jewel of US spaceports, is aging and approaching its limit due to increased demand from private companies, including SpaceX and Blue Origin, a new report finds.

“NASA’s launch infrastructure is vital to providing the agency, other government agencies, and commercial partners access to space for their most complex and expensive missions,” states the report, published by the NASA Office of Inspector General. “Nevertheless, NASA’s launch infrastructure is dated and often does not provide the capacity to meet the growing demands of the agency and its partners.”

The report covers NASA’s launch facilities at the Kennedy Space Center in Florida and Wallops Flight Facility in Virginia. However, the most noteworthy information in the report concerns the Florida spaceport, where demand from SpaceX’s Starship and Blue Origin’s New Glenn launch vehicles is expected to stress NASA.

NASA only has a handful of launch pads at Kennedy. Launch Complex 39A is currently leased by SpaceX for Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy launches, and also houses a new launch facility that will soon support Starship launches. Launch Complex 39B is home to NASA’s Space Launch System rocket, and Launch Complex 39C has not been used due to its proximity to this pad. Finally, NASA has built a 10-acre site, Launch Complex 48, that it may lease to small launch vehicle companies.
The space agency’s neighbor to the south, Cape Canaveral Space Force Station, has many more pads. This includes the Launch Complex 36A and 36B leased to Blue Origin for the New Glenn rocket.

Although the Space Force manages its own launch facilities, the military works closely with NASA and they share some responsibilities. Most critically, there are supply lines for helium and nitrogen, as well as 231 miles of paved roads and bridges that serve both Kennedy and Cape Canaveral. Additionally, the report cites serious concerns about a six-decade-old electricity distribution system for NASA’s launch pads.

One example of overcapacity cited by the report concerns the availability of gaseous nitrogen used during the fueling, testing, and launching of rockets. During the Artemis I launch campaign in 2022, there were issues with the availability of enough nitrogen to support the rocket. But according to the report, the problem has not gone away.

“The system cannot simultaneously support launches … of Blue Origin’s New Glenn launch vehicle at Space Launch Complex 36 and United Launch Alliance’s Vulcan Centaur launch vehicle at Space Launch Complex 41,” the report states. “Blue Origin officials stated this issue created a major scheduling challenge during preparation for the New Glenn-1 mission that launched in January 2025, and further expressed concern that during future Space Launch System launches there could be 1- to 2-month blackout periods from the pipeline.”
The solution for this is to construct a new gaseous nitrogen system to supplement the existing capacity, but the $25 million project is currently unfunded.

Officials are also concerned that the number of annual launches, in addition to major test firings, will reach or exceed the number of days in a year by late 2028 or 2029, placing “significant strain” on Kennedy’s spaceport systems. And increasingly, these will come from rockets, putting the most stress on roads and requiring the most commodities for launch.

SpaceX has told NASA it plans to launch Starship every eight days from Launch Complex 39A for the purposes of propellant depots in orbit. (The report states, in a footnote, that “at least” 15 Starships will be required to deliver propellant for a Starship lunar lander.) From all of its Florida pads, SpaceX estimates 120 annual Starship launches, and Blue Origin projects 120 annual launches of its super heavy-lift New Glenn rocket as well by 2035.
keyPoints: SpaceX has told NASA it plans to launch Starship every eight days from Kennedy.

NASA’s infrastructure at Kennedy Space Center, the crown jewel of US spaceports, is aging and approaching its limit due to increased demand from private companies, including SpaceX and Blue Origin, a new report finds.

“NASA’s launch infrastructure is vital to providing the agency, other government agencies, and commercial partners access to space for their most complex and expensive missions,” states the report, published by the NASA Office of Inspector General. “Nevertheless, NASA’s launch infrastructure is dated and often does not provide the capacity to meet the growing demands of the agency and its partners.”
paragraphTranslations: SpaceX已经告诉美国宇航局（NASA），它计划每8天从肯尼迪航天中心发射一次星舰。
美国宇航局在肯尼迪航天中心的基础设施，这个美国航天发射场的皇冠上的明珠，由于包括SpaceX和蓝色起源在内的私营公司的增加需求，正在老化并接近其极限，一份新报告发现。
“美国宇航局的发射基础设施对于为该机构、其他政府机构和商业合作伙伴提供进入太空的途径至关重要，用于他们最复杂和最昂贵的任务，”由美国宇航局监察长办公室发布的报告指出。“尽管如此，美国宇航局的发射基础设施已经过时，往往无法提供满足该机构及其合作伙伴日益增长需求的能力。”
该报告涵盖了美国宇航局在佛罗里达州肯尼迪航天中心和弗吉尼亚州沃洛普斯飞行设施的发射设施。然而，报告中最值得注意的信息涉及佛罗里达的航天发射场，预计SpaceX的星舰和蓝色起源的新格伦发射载具的需求将给美国宇航局带来压力。
美国宇航局在肯尼迪只有少数几个发射台。39A发射复合体目前由SpaceX租赁，用于猎鹰9号和猎鹰重型发射，并且还设有一个新的发射设施，不久将支持星舰发射。39B发射复合体是NASA太空发射系统火箭的所在地，由于靠近这个发射台，39C发射复合体尚未使用。最后，NASA建造了一个10英亩的场地，48号发射复合体，它可能会租给小型发射载具公司。
美国宇航局南部的邻居，卡纳维拉尔角太空部队基地有更多的发射台。这包括蓝色起源为新格伦火箭租赁的36A和36B发射复合体。
尽管太空部队管理自己的发射设施，但军方与NASA密切合作，他们共享一些责任。最关键的是，有供应氦气和氮气的供应链，以及231英里的铺装道路和桥梁，服务于肯尼迪和卡纳维拉尔角。此外，报告还提到了对美国宇航局发射台60年历史的电力分配系统的严重担忧。
报告中提到超负荷的一个例子是，在火箭加油、测试和发射期间使用的气态氮的可用性。在2022年的阿尔忒弥斯一号发射活动中，出现了足够的氮气供应问题。但根据报告，这个问题并没有消失。
“该系统无法同时支持在36号太空发射复合体的蓝色起源新格伦发射载具和在41号太空发射复合体的联合发射联盟的火神半人马座发射载具的发射……”报告指出。“蓝色起源官员表示，这个问题在准备2025年1月发射的新格伦-1任务期间造成了重大的调度挑战，并进一步表达了在未来太空发射系统发射期间，从管道可能会有1至2个月的停电期的担忧。”
解决这个问题的方法是建造一个新的气态氮系统来补充现有的容量，但目前这个2500万美元的项目尚未获得资金。
官员们还担心，到2028年或2029年底，每年的发射次数，加上主要的测试点火，将达到或超过一年的天数，对肯尼迪航天发射场系统造成“重大压力”。而且，这些将越来越多地来自火箭，对道路造成最大的压力，并需要最多的发射商品。
SpaceX已经告诉NASA，它计划每8天从39A发射复合体发射一次星舰，用于轨道上的推进剂库。（报告在脚注中指出，“至少”需要15艘星舰才能为星舰月球着陆器运送推进剂。）从其在佛罗里达的所有发射台，SpaceX估计每年将有120次星舰发射，而蓝色起源也预计到2035年每年将有120次其超重型新格伦火箭的发射。

vocabulary.example: The city's infrastructure is aging.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 这座城市的基础设施正在老化。

vocabulary.example: The demand for new housing is increasing.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 对新住房的需求正在增加。

vocabulary.example: The company is leasing office space in the city center.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 公司在市中心租用了办公空间。

vocabulary.example: The rocket launch is scheduled for next month.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 火箭发射计划在下个月进行。

vocabulary.example: The availability of water is a major concern in this region.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 水资源的可用性是这个地区的主要关切。

vocabulary.example: The company holds an annual meeting to review the year.
vocabulary.exampleZh: 公司每年举行年会以回顾一年的情况。

paragraphs: A report by NASA's Office of Inspector General warns that the Kennedy Space Center's infrastructure is aging and can't meet the increasing demand from private companies like SpaceX and Blue Origin. The center has limited launch pads, with SpaceX leasing one for its Starship launches. The report also highlights concerns about the electricity distribution system and the availability of gaseous nitrogen for rocket launches. SpaceX plans to launch Starship every eight days, and both SpaceX and Blue Origin expect to have 120 annual launches by 2035.
paragraphTranslations: NASA监察长办公室的一份报告警告说，肯尼迪航天中心的基础设施正在老化，无法满足SpaceX和Blue Origin等私营公司日益增长的需求。该中心的发射台数量有限，SpaceX租用了一个用于其星舰发射。报告还强调了对电力分配系统和火箭发射中使用的液态氮供应的担忧。SpaceX计划每八天发射一次星舰，预计到2035年，SpaceX和Blue Origin都将每年发射120次。
